purpose: Pre-registered a-priori tagging of each defect subtype as either 'distinctive' (semantically identifiable — broken, missing part, contamination, large crack) or 'subtle' (within-category variation — color shift, minor bend, surface roughness). Tagged BEFORE Pass 3/4 runs so post-hoc cherry-picking is impossible. Used to test the hypothesis: 'N=30 fine-tune works when defect is distinctive; fails when defect is subtle.'
methodology: Tagging based on each subtype's VISUAL character from the dataset's public examples, without reference to any training/eval result. A subtype is 'distinctive' if a naive human could describe it in one phrase (e.g. 'hole in the shell', 'missing component'); 'subtle' if description requires quantitative reference ('slightly darker', 'minor bend'). Inevitably subjective at the margin; documented here exactly as committed.

candle.rationale: dark spots, chunks of wax, missing wicks are visually identifiable

capsules.rationale: bubbles/discolor are subtle; misshape/scratches are distinctive — roughly 50/50

chewinggum.rationale: cracks, missing regions, color shifts are clear

fryum.rationale: broken parts, burns, missing pieces are clear; surface_roughness is subtle

macaroni1.rationale: small color variations, minor cracks, similarity to normal pasta variation

macaroni2.rationale: similar to macaroni1 — pasta surface variations dominate

pcb1.rationale: missing components, wrong components are structural

hypothesis_to_test: Pass 4 (Tier A completion at N=30) — AUROC on distinctive categories exceeds 0.85 on at least 80% of cats. AUROC on subtle categories stays below 0.80 regardless of intervention, and reproduces metal_nut's degenerate F1 pattern on a subset. Mixed categories scatter in between. If this prediction holds across 27 categories, the tiger-analogy taxonomy is validated as a predictive rule and becomes a paper contribution.
notes: Pass 1 results (post-hoc check against taxonomy): hazelnut (distinctive) 0.949, candle (distinctive) 0.956, DeepPCB (distinctive) 0.835, bottle (distinctive) 0.672, fryum (distinctive) 0.828, pcb1 (distinctive) 0.748, metal_nut (subtle) 0.731-degenerate. 6/7 distinctive categories reach AUROC > 0.67; 1/1 subtle category is degenerate. Consistent with the hypothesis but N is too small to confirm.
bottle's relatively weak lift (0.513 → 0.672) despite distinctive label is worth noting — broken_small variant may be dragging the average. Could subset to broken_large + contamination only to test.
The taxonomy is deliberately binary; in practice each category is a mixture. Paper should report per-category AND stratified-by-tag numbers so reviewers can inspect the grouping.
